content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

May 8, 1990

Lois M. Shade  
811 East Juanita  
Glendora, CA 91740

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-288

Dear Ms. Shade:

This is in response to your letter of April 19, 1990, requesting confirmation of telephone advice concerning the use of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Your letter correctly summarizes my advice that your campaign funds may be used to pay air fare to Sacramento to participate in an advocacy program with the East San Gabriel Valley Republican Women, in which you agreed to participate prior to the election, notwithstanding the fact that you lost your bid for reelection to the Glendora City Council. A candidate2 may use campaign funds to pay travel expenses if such expenses are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 85802(a).)

The Act limits the use of surplus funds raised after January 1, 1989. However, funds do not become surplus funds until the later of leaving elected office or the end of the postelection reporting period following the defeat of a candidate for elective office. (Section 85807.)

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Under the Act, an individual who becomes a candidate shall retain his or her status as a candidate until such time as that status is terminated pursuant to Section 84214. (Section 82007.)

If you have any questions regarding this advice, you may contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division
